Dr. Adil Sarıbay and Assoc. Prof. Dr. Onurcan Yılmaz: Thinking and Intuition in a World Full of Crises: Thinking Too Much or Not Thinking at All

The book ‘Reflection and Intuition in a Crisis-Ridden World: Thinking Hard or Hardly Thinking’ written by Prof. Dr. Adil Sarıbay, Head of the Department of Psychology, and Assoc. Prof. Dr. Onurcan Yılmaz, Director of MINT Lab, is out. Prof. Dr. Jonathan Baron and Prof. Dr. Keith E. Stanovich, two renowned researchers known for their work in the field of psychology, wrote the back cover of the book.

Jonathan Baron, Professor Emeritus of Psychology, School of Arts and Sciences, University of Pennsylvania, USA

“A very interesting and wide-ranging survey of current thinking about deep thinking and intuition and their relation to public affairs. It has a special place in recognizing the connections with social psychology, which is often neglected in the literature and often unfamiliar territory for me.”

Keith E. Stanovich, Emeritus Professor of Applied Psychology and Human Development, University of Toronto, Canada

“The field of cognitive science is making a major contribution thanks to this book, which extends and deepens the concept of cognitive deep thinking into far-reaching areas that are politically sensitive and fraught with real-world implications. It takes deep thinking out of the laboratory and connects it to urgent cultural and political debates.”
